title: 10 best herbal essential oils for constipation
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for constipation are natural remedies derived from plants that help to alleviate symptoms of constipation, such as infrequent bowel movements, bloating, and discomfort. These essential oils work by stimulating digestion, relaxing the muscles in the intestinal tract, and reducing inflammation. The best herbal essential oils for constipation include Foeniculum vulgare, Zingiber officinale, Cassia angustifolia, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Angelica archangelica, Ficus carica, Rheum officinale, Peumus boldus, Silybum marianum, and Mentha x piperita. If left untreated, constipation can lead to fatigue, weight loss, and decreased quality of life, causing individuals to miss work, school, or experience social isolation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with constipation because of its natural lubricating properties that soothe and relax the digestive tract. The oil's anise-like aroma stimulates digestion, reducing inflammation and spasms in the colon that can contribute to constipation. Additionally, fennel essential oil's carminative properties help expel gas and bloating, allowing stool to pass more easily through the intestines. By promoting regular bowel movements and easing digestive discomfort, fennel essential oil provides natural relief from constipation symptoms.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Fennel essential oil helps to relieve bloating, gas, and abdominal discomfort associated with constipation by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing spasms.

Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to constipation, and help to soothe and calm the mucous membranes lining the gut.
Stimulant property: Fennel essential oil's stimulant properties increase intestinal motility, helping to move food through the digestive system more efficiently and prevent or alleviate constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant contain compounds that can help stimulate digestive enzymes and improve bowel function, making them an effective part for treating constipation.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including constipation, due to their ability to relax the muscles in the intestinal tract and increase digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems in the morning for optimal potency.
Wash the harvested plant material with distilled water to remove impurities thoroughly.
Steam distill the plant material using a still or a mini-distiller at atmospheric pressure slowly.
Collect the essential oil as it condenses into a container at room temperature promptly.
Filter the collected essential oil through paper filter or cheesecloth to remove sediment quickly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood essential oils helps with constipation because of its unique ability to stimulate digestion and relieve intestinal discomfort. The oil's active compounds, such as absinthin and thujone, have been shown to increase gut motility, reducing the risk of constipation and promoting regular bowel movements. Additionally, wormwood essential oils has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to chronic constipation. This natural remedy is a safe and effective way to maintain a healthy digestive system.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Wormwood essential oil's antispasmodic properties can help relax and soothe the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to constipation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of wormwood essential oil can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to constipation by slowing down digestion and bowel movements.
Stimulant properties: Wormwood essential oil's stimulant properties can help stimulate bowel movements and increase appetite, both of which can be helpful in alleviating symptoms of constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wormwood roots contain sesquiterpenes, which help to stimulate digestive enzymes and relieve constipation.
Barks: Wormwood barks have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including constipation, due to their ability to relax the muscles in the intestines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried artemisia absinthium leaves in the early morning for optimal oil quality.
Chop the harvested leaves into small pieces to increase surface area and facilitate steam distillation.
Combine one part chopped artemisia absinthium leaves with three parts distilled water in a stainless steel steam distillation apparatus.
Distill the mixture using high-pressure steam for 1-2 hours or until the oil yield reaches 2-5%.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or paper filter to remove impurities and debris.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica essential oils helps with constipation because of its natural properties that stimulate digestion and relieve bloating. The oil contains a compound called asarone, which has been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, increasing bowel movements and reducing symptoms of constipation. Additionally, angelica's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe the gut lining, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with constipation. By promoting regular digestion and reducing inflammation, angelica essential oils can provide natural relief from constipation.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Angelica essential oil helps to expel gas from the digestive tract, reducing bloating and discomfort that often accompanies constipation.

Antispasmodic property: The oil's antispasmodic effects can help relieve cramps and spasms in the intestinal muscles, making it easier for stool to pass through the colon and alleviating constipation symptoms.
Stimulant property: Angelica essential oil has a mild stimulating effect on the digestive system, which can help increase bowel motility and encourage regular bowel movements.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Angelica are rich in volatile oils that help to stimulate bowel movements and relieve symptoms of constipation.
Barks: The barks of Angelica contain compounds that help to soften stool, making it easier to pass and providing relief from constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica leaves and stems at dawn for optimal potency.
Wash the harvested material thoroughly with distilled water to remove impurities.
Steam distill the cleaned plant material using 10-15% distillation ratio for 30-40 minutes.
Collect the obtained essential oil in a glass container and let it settle for 24 hours.
Filter the oil through a coffee filter or a paper towel to remove any sediment or impurities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with constipation because it has a soothing and calming effect on the digestive system, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate constipation. Additionally, lemon balm's natural anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to constipation. Soothing and calming effect: Lemon balm oil's gentle, calming properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of constipation, promoting a more relaxed digestive system and regular bowel habits.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm essential oil contains antioxidants that help protect the gut lining from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to constipation, improving overall gut health and function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant are rich in antioxidants and have a calming effect, which can help soothe digestive issues related to constipation.
Leaves: The leaves contain compounds like citral and geraniol, which have natural laxative properties that can help stimulate bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves and stems from well-maintained gardens.
Dry the harvested plant material using a warm air circulation method for 24 hours.
Combine one part of dried melissa officinalis with three parts of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil.
Steam distill the mixture in an all-copper still using 200ml of water and 400g of plant material for 2-3 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon essential oils helps with constipation because of its natural digestive properties. It has been traditionally used to soothe the stomach and intestines, stimulating bowel movements and reducing symptoms of constipation such as bloating and discomfort. The oil's warming and carminative effects also help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing for smoother passage of stool and a reduction in straining during bowel movements. Regular use of Ceylon cinnamon essential oils may promote regularity and ease digestion, providing relief from constipation symptoms.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to constipation. This allows for easier passage of stool and relief from discomfort.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds present in Ceylon cinnamon essential oil help to reduce inflammation in the intestines, which can exacerbate constipation, while also protecting against oxidative stress and damage that can lead to chronic digestive issues.

Stimulant property: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil has a gentle stimulating effect on the digestive system, increasing contractions and peristalsis, which helps to move food through the digestive tract more efficiently, reducing constipation symptoms such as bloating and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in cinnamic acid, a natural laxative compound that helps stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
Barks: Contain cinnamon oil, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe digestive issues and reduce symptoms of constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinnamomum verum bark and dried it in the shade for at least two weeks.
Grind 100 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark into fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine ground cinnamomum verum bark with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Steam distill the mixture in a double boiler for 2-3 hours until the essential oil is extracted and separated from the solids.
Filter the essential oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with constipation because its unique properties stimulate digestive function, easing bowel movements and alleviating symptoms of chronic constipation. The oil's anti-inflammatory effects soothe irritated intestinal walls, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with constipation. Additionally, maidenhair tree essential oil's natural laxative properties help to soften stool, making it easier to pass, thus promoting regular and healthy bowel movements. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive system, which can contribute to constipation.
Choleretic properties: The essential oil has been shown to stimulate bile production and secretion, which helps improve digestion, absorption of nutrients, and bowel movements.

Soothing and anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's essential oil contains compounds that have a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and irritation in the gut, which can alleviate symptoms of constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including constipation.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help soothe and calm the digestive tract, relieving symptoms of constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and clean them thoroughly by rinsing under cool running water.
Steam distill the cleaned ginkgo biloba leaves using 100g of leaves for every 500ml of water at 100°c for 30 minutes.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water through centrifugation or decantation and filter it using a coffee filter paper.
Mix the filtered essential oil with carrier oils such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil in a ratio of 1:10 for easier application.
Store the ginkgo biloba essential oil blend in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and keep at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway essential oils helps with constipation because of its natural digestive properties that stimulate the muscles in the intestines, promoting regular bowel movements. The oil's carvone compound has been shown to relax spasms and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, allowing stool to pass more easily. Additionally, caraway oil's anti-inflammatory effects can help soothe irritated bowels and relieve symptoms of constipation such as bloating and discomfort. By using herbal caraway essential oils topically or aromatically, individuals can experience relief from constipation and promote a healthy digestive system.
remedy_properties: Choleretic property: Caraway essential oil helps stimulate bile production and flow, which can aid in breaking down food and relieving constipation by reducing stagnation and improving digestion.

Anti-inflammatory property: Caraway essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to constipation, and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort.

Carminative property: Caraway essential oil has natural carminative properties that can help relieve flatulence, bloating, and discomfort associated with constipation by reducing gas and alleviating spasms in the digestive tract.

Seeds: Caraway seeds contain a compound called cumic acid, which has natural laxative properties that help to soften stool and promote bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh carum carvi seeds and grind them into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of ground carum carvi seeds with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil.
Steam distill the mixture for 4-6 hours to extract the essential oils from the seeds.
Filter the resulting liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and sediment.
Store the carum carvi essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, away from direct sunlight, at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with constipation because of its natural analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and ease discomfort associated with bowel movements. The oil's menthol content also has a cooling effect on the intestines, increasing blood flow and stimulating contractions that help move stool through the colon more efficiently, promoting regular bowel movements and relieving constipation symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves and dry them using a dehydrator or air drying method for 24 hours.
Crush the dried peppermint leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area and release their oils.
Combine the crushed peppermint leaves with carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1:4 and let it steep for 8 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the essential oil from the solids and discard the solids.
Store the mentha x piperita essential oil in a dark glass bottle with an airtight lid and keep it refrigerated for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime essential oils helps with constipation because it has a profound impact on digestion, stimulating bowel movements and relieving symptoms of chronic constipation. The oil's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing for smoother movement of stool through the intestines. remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia platyphyllos flowers in full bloom and dry them thoroughly using a food dehydrator at 55 degrees celsius for eight hours.
Combine two cups of dried tilia platyphyllos flowers with one cup of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil in a glass jar.
Place the jar in a dark cupboard for three days to allow the mixture to steep and infuse, shaking it daily.
After the steeping process, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Store the resulting essential oil in a cool dark place for up to six months or use immediately as desired, using a few drops diluted with carrier oil for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saffron essential oils helps with constipation because of its natural digestive properties. The oil's active compounds, such as safranal and crocin, have been shown to stimulate the muscles in the digestive tract, promoting regular bowel movements and reducing symptoms of constipation. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality crocus sativus flowers and leaves in the early morning hours for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a clean dry cloth or machine for at least two weeks.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a clevenger apparatus or similar equipment for 4-6 hours.
Combine the resulting essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil in a 1:5 ratio and store in dark glass containers.
Allow the mixture to mature for at least 24 hours before filtering and bottling for use in aromatherapy blends.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat constipation with herbal essential oils is to combine a few drops of oils like peppermint, ginger, and fennel with a carrier oil and massage it onto the abdomen. This stimulates digestion and relieves symptoms such as bloating and discomfort. Alternatively, inhaling the steam from a bowl of hot water mixed with these oils can also help stimulate bowel movements and alleviate constipation. Regular use can lead to improved regularity and overall digestive health.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to constipation that are treated with herbal essential oils are digestive disorders such as bloating, gas, and indigestion. Certain herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and fennel have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the stomach and ease symptoms of IBS (Irritable Bowel Syndrome). remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils helps with constipation because it has carminative and laxative properties that ease digestive issues. The oil's active compounds, such as anethole and fenchone, have a soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and promoting smooth bowel movements. Additionally, Foeniculum vulgare essential oils stimulate the muscles in the intestines, helping to relieve symptoms of constipation and promote a regular bowel function. This makes it a popular natural remedy for constipation relief.
remedy_constituents: Trans-Anethole: Acts as a carminative agent to help alleviate constipation by reducing gas and promoting digestive motility.
Foeniculin: Exhibits laxative properties, helping to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
Bisabolol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation that may contribute to constipation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of fenchone, a compound that helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and promote bowel movement.
Seeds: Utilized because they are rich in essential oils, particularly anethole, which has a carminative effect, helping to relieve constipation by reducing gas and bloating.
Fruits: Employed due to their anise-flavored essential oil content, which has a laxative effect and helps to stimulate bowel movement, making them useful in treating constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare stalks when they are mature and the seeds are brown and dry at least 2 weeks after flowering.
Dry the harvested stalks in a warm place or using a dehydrator at 40°c for 2-3 days.
Distill the dried stalks using a steam distillation apparatus at 100°c for 3-4 hours to obtain a foeniculum vulgare essential oil.
Filter the foeniculum vulgare essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and sediment.
Bottle the filtered foeniculum vulgare essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with constipation because it stimulates digestive function and improves gut motility, promoting the regular passage of bowel movements. The oil's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to constipation. By enhancing digestive efficiency and reducing discomfort, Zingiber officinale essential oils can help alleviate constipation symptoms and support a healthy digestive system. Regular use may also improve overall digestive function.
remedy_constituents: Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene helps with constipation by relaxing smooth muscle in the digestive tract, increasing gut motility and reducing inflammation that can contribute to constipation.
Gingerol: This phenolic compound helps with constipation by reducing inflammation and improving gut motility, which can help move food through the digestive system more efficiently.

Shogaol: This phenolic compound helps with constipation by relaxing smooth muscle in the digestive tract and reducing inflammation, which can help alleviate constipation symptoms and improve gut motility.
remedy_parts: Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale contain essential oils that help stimulate digestion and relieve constipation by improving gut motility and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes preferably in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Steam distill the harvested material at 100-110°c for 3-4 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separation funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove any impurities or debris.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at a controlled temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia angustifolia essential oils helps with constipation because of its natural laxative properties and ability to stimulate bowel movements. The oil is rich in anthraquinone glycosides, which are known to soften and bulk up stool, making it easier to pass. Additionally, Cassia angustifolia essential oils can help to relax the muscles in the intestines, allowing for a smoother and more efficient elimination process, providing relief from constipation and promoting a healthy digestive system. It has been used for centuries as a natural remedy.
remedy_constituents: Sennosides: Sennosides are anthraquinone glycosides that stimulate bowel movements and help relieve constipation by inducing bowel contractions and increasing water and electrolyte absorption in the colon.

Anthraquinones (including Aloe-emodin and Chrysophanol): Anthraquinones have laxative properties that help move stool through the digestive system by increasing intestinal motility, relaxing the intestinal muscles, and stimulating the colon to contract and move stool.

Flavonoids (including Quercetin): Flavonoids possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the gut lining, reduce inflammation, and promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which can help alleviate constipation and improve overall gut health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary part used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of sennosides, which are potent laxatives.
Seeds: Seeds are also used to produce essential oils, which contain anthraquinone glycosides that help stimulate bowel movements.
Roots: Roots are another part used to make essential oils, as they contain sennosides and anthraquinone glycosides that help alleviate constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cassia angustifolia leaves and flowers at the peak of potency to ensure high quality essential oil.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a distillation apparatus for 3 to 4 hours to extract oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation apparatus using a separatory funnel and collect the oil in a clean container.
Filter the essential oil through a filter paper to remove any impurities and improve its clarity and quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with constipation because it stimulates digestive enzymes and improves gut motility. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, allowing for smoother passage of food and waste. remedy_constituents: Liquiritin: It helps with constipation by stimulating bowel movements and improving gut motility due to its ability to relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract.

Licoricidin: It helps with constipation by relaxing the intestinal muscles and improving the movement of food through the digestive system, allowing for more efficient elimination of waste.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make essential oils because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that stimulates bowel movements and relieves constipation.

Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make essential oils because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the digestive system, promoting regular bowel movements.
Seeds: The seeds of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make essential oils because they have carminative properties that help relieve gas and bloating, which can contribute to constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots at dawn when dew is still present on the surface.
Dry the roots in a well-ventilated area at 35°c for 3 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried roots at 100°c for 4 hours to release essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the water distillate using a separating funnel and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica essential oils helps with constipation because it has natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties that soothe and relax the digestive system. remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Angelica archangelica are used to make essential oils for constipation because they contain angelic root oil, which has been traditionally used to stimulate digestion and relieve constipation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal quality.

Steam distill the dried plant material at a temperature not exceeding 100 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours to extract essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ficus carica essential oils helps with constipation because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the digestive system and promote regular bowel movements. remedy_constituents: Furanocoumarins: These compounds help stimulate bowel movements and improve gut motility, alleviating constipation.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, possess laxative properties and help to soften stool, making it easier to pass.
Tannins: Tannins have a mild laxative effect due to their ability to increase gut water content and stimulate bowel movements, reducing constipation symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ficus carica are used to make essential oils for constipation due to their potential laxative and purgative properties.
Fruits: The fruits of Ficus carica are used to make essential oils for constipation due to their high fiber content and potential to aid in digestion.
Barks: The barks of Ficus carica are used to make essential oils for constipation due to their potential anti-inflammatory and laxative properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh ficus carica fruit at peak ripeness when the skin turns from green to purple brown color.
Steam distill the fresh fruit in a still for 2 hours at 100°c using 2 liters of distilled water.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol by decantation or centrifugation.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron membrane to remove any impurities or particles.
Store the ficus carica essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid at 4°c.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rheum officinale essential oils helps with constipation because it stimulates the digestive system and promotes bowel movements. The oil contains compounds such as rhaponticin and rhapontigenin, which have been shown to increase the movement of the intestines and relieve symptoms of constipation. Anthraquinones: Anthraquinones have a cathartic effect, causing the muscles in the intestines to contract and move food through the digestive system more quickly, relieving constipation.

Emodin: Emodin helps to increase the movement of food through the digestive system, promoting bowel movements and reducing the risk of constipation by stimulating the intestinal muscles and improving gut motility.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used for their anthraquinone content, which stimulates bowel movements and helps relieve constipation.
Leaves: Leaves contain anthraquinones and other compounds that help soften stool and stimulate bowel movements, alleviating constipation.
Roots: Roots are used for their high concentration of anthraquinones, which have a laxative effect and help relieve constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh rheum officinale roots in the early morning to avoid moisture content variations.
Dry the roots in a well-ventilated area at 30c for 7 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried roots at 100c for 3 hours to extract essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separator and collect 10ml of oil.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove impurities and contaminants.

remedies.remedy_desc: Peumus boldus essential oils helps with constipation because it is a natural laxative that stimulates bowel movements, relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, and increases the frequency of bowel movements. The oil's unique blend of compounds, including boldine and scopoletin, helps to improve gut health by enhancing digestive enzyme activity and reducing inflammation in the intestines. As a result, Peumus boldus essential oils can provide relief from constipation and promote a regular and healthy bowel routine.
remedy_constituents: Boldine: Acts as a smooth muscle relaxant, helping to relax the muscles in the intestines and improve bowel movements.
Quercetin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and promoting a healthy gut environment that supports regular bowel movements.
Isoboldine: Displays cholagogic properties, stimulating the release of bile from the gallbladder and improving digestion, which can help alleviate constipation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for Peumus boldus essential oil due to their high concentration of active compounds that help alleviate constipation.
Roots: The roots of Peumus boldus are also used for their rich content of active ingredients that aid in digestion and relieve constipation.
Barks: The barks are another used part for the essential oil, contributing to its therapeutic properties that help manage constipation and related digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh boldus leaves in large quantities from mature plants for optimal oil yield.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator for 2 days.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a distillation apparatus with water at 100°c for 2 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus and store it in dark glass bottles.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove impurities and improve quality immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils helps with constipation because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the digestive tract, promoting regular bowel movements. The oil's flavonoids and phenolic acids stimulate the gut, enhancing intestinal motility and reducing inflammation, which can alleviate constipation symptoms. Additionally, Silybum marianum oil has been shown to protect the gut lining, preventing damage from toxins and promoting a healthy gut microbiome, further contributing to improved digestion and constipation relief.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help alleviate constipation by increasing the contractility of the intestinal muscles and improving gut motility.
Lignans: Lignans in Silybum marianum help regulate bowel movements and alleviate constipation by promoting the relaxation of intestinal smooth muscle and enhancing gut secretion.
Triterpenes: Triterpenes, particularly ursolic acid, exhibit laxative properties, helping to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation by increasing the movement of intestinal contents.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make essential oils due to their potential to stimulate digestion and relieve constipation.
Leaves: They are used to make essential oils for their possible anti-inflammatory and laxative properties.
Flowers: They are used to make essential oils due to their potential to stimulate digestion and relieve constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried silybum marianum flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm and well-ventilated area for 2-3 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a distillation apparatus with a 4:1 water to plant material ratio for 4-6 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel or decantation method.
Filter the collected essential oil through a glass wool or paper filter to remove any impurities and contaminants.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils helps with constipation because it has a stimulating effect on the digestive system. The oil's active compound, menthol, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease digestive discomfort and promote bowel movements. Additionally, menthol's ability to relax the muscles in the intestinal tract can help stimulate contractions, making it easier to pass stool. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate constipation and promote a healthy digestive system.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps to increase intestinal motility and reduce spasms, making it easier to pass stool and relieve constipation.
Pulegone: Exhibits a relaxing effect on smooth muscle in the digestive system, which can help to alleviate constipation by improving bowel movement.
Menthone: Possesses a mild laxative effect, which helps to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used to make peppermint essential oil, which has a strong digestive property that helps relieve constipation due to its carminative and antispasmodic effects.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are also used to make essential oil that may aid in relieving constipation by promoting digestion and easing stomach discomfort.
Roots: The roots are used in traditional herbal medicine for their carminative and antispasmodic properties, which help to relieve constipation by promoting digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Clean the harvested plant material by gently washing it with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities.
Steam distill the clean plant material at a temperature range of 100-150°f for 2-3 hours to release oils.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation process using a separator or a condenser to separate it from water.
Store the obtained essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight and heat sources to preserve its quality.
